content.full_text:
June 6, 1977

Mr. James R. Christiansen  
City Attorney, Carpinteria  
1215 State Street  
Santa Barbara, California 93103

Dear Mr. Christiansen:

This is in response to your letters to the Commission dated 10 May and 20 May 1977 concerning a possible conflict of interest involving Mayor Monte Widders. This letter should be treated as informal advice of the Commission pursuant to Government Code §83114(b) and 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329. I trust it will enable you to resolve the situation you describe.

The basic prohibitions in the Political Reform Act against conflicts of interest are found at Government Code §§87100 and 87103, which provide, in relevant part: 1/

87100. No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest.

87103. An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on:

1/The City Council of the City of Carpinteria has also adopted a conflict of interest code pursuant to Government Code §87300, et seq. Its disqualification provisions reiterate and track the language of the cited code sections.
